untranslatable_reason: Signal requires multi-leg spread analysis between two futures contracts (front month vs back month), calculation of theoretical spread based on storage/insurance/financing costs, and deviation measurement from equilibrium. The allowed indicators are single-instrument technical indicators and cannot model inter-contract spread relationships, cost basis calculations, or theoretical fair value computations. This requires a custom spread calculator outside the scope of single-bar technical analysis.
translation_notes: Calendar spread strategies require dedicated spread pricing models and dual-contract data feeds. The signal references specific parameters (storage_cost, insurance_cost, financing_cost, theoretical_spread_range) that cannot be derived from standard price/volume indicators. A true implementation would need: (1) dual-contract data handler, (2) cost-of-carry calculator, (3) spread deviation quantifier, (4) mean-reversion detector. The provided placeholder uses price mean reversion as a weak proxy, but this does not capture the actual calendar spread logic described in the source material.
